summary: Firey China>Rider, seamless Wheel→Gimme Some Lovin', inspiring Lost Sailor > Saint
blurb: The Greek Theatre show shines with a smoking China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and a tight Wheel → Gimme Some Lovin' transition. The second set delivers an inspired Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance and a soulful She Belongs To Me encore. Overall the band is locked in, with standout keyboard textures and powerful bass.
review: June 15 1985 at Berkeley’s Greek Theatre finds the Dead firing on all cylinders. Jerry’s lead work is fast‑yet‑focused, his vocal phrasing on “She Belongs To Me” and the China→Rider segue is both emotional and precise. Phil’s bass thunders through “Northbound Train” moments and adds a deep, rumbling foundation to every jam, even though one matrix source notes a slight post‑mix reduction. Brent Mydland supplies his trademark lush intro on China Cat and layers that lift Lost Sailor and the Wheel into new heights; reviewers call him "on fire" for these cuts. The dual drummers drive an energetic second set—Mickey and Billy are spectacular most of the night, with only a few hiccups during “Big River” and “U.S. Blues.” The soundboard recordings capture this brilliance with crisp separation (despite minor pitch quirks), making this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that 1985 peak.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Eight of nine recordings are overwhelmingly positive, praising the China>Rider sequence, second‑set jams, and overall band tightness; only one mixed review notes a minor glitch, so strong positive sentiment yields a +1.6 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard with clear, crisp mix and excellent instrument separation; captures all key moments despite slight pitch anomalies and missing encore tracks.

key_highlights: Smoking China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue
Seamless Wheel → Gimme Some Lovin' transition
Inspired Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance jam
Soulful She Belongs To Me encore

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers fast‑yet‑focused lead work, belts out lyrics on China>Rider and sings with soulful emotion on She Belongs To Me.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a powerful, "PHAT" bass tone that drives the jams; his low‑end rumble shines throughout, though one matrix source notes slight post‑mix reduction.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds solid rhythm guitar and strong vocal contributions, with reviewers noting perfect vocals on Lost Sailor and U.S. Blues.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s intro on China Cat is iconic; his lush, trippy layers enhance Lost Sailor, the Wheel, and Gimme Some Lovin', earning praise as "on fire" for this show.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart fuels the high‑energy second set with spectacular, driving drumming, despite occasional tightness hiccups on a few songs.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann matches Hart’s intensity, delivering an overall loose yet energetic feel; his work helps propel the jam-heavy second set.
